target.title: fix: strip volatile output from exec result hash to fix no-progress detection (#93917)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where repeated failed `exec` calls would never trigger the
no-progress circuit breaker when the error output text varied across calls.
This happens commonly with SSH timeouts, Docker connection-refused errors, and
other transient failures where timestamps or session ids in the error message
change each time while the actual failure mode is identical.

Fixes #93917

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.ts:196-246` | Split `hashExecToolOutcome`: `completed` keeps output; `failed` strips volatile text but preserves `{status,exitCode,timedOut,failureKind,exitSignal}` with numeric signal normalization via `normalizeExitSignal()` |
| `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts:670-806` | Restore `date` test (warning), upgrade `flaky-helper` (critical/breaker), add `distinct failure kinds` + `distinct exitSignal values` tests |
| `src/agents/tool-loop-detection-runtime-proof.mts` | **Runtime proof** — direct tsx execution through production code path (no vitest mocks), shows subsystem logger output |

## Why This Change Was Made

Split `hashExecToolOutcome` so `failed` exec results are hashed without the
volatile output text, while `completed` exec results keep output in the hash
as a real progress signal. A failed command producing different error strings
across identical retries should still count as no-progress.

## User Impact

Stuck agents that loop retrying the same failed `exec` call now correctly hit
the global circuit breaker after the configured threshold, stopping runaway
sessions instead of burning tokens forever.

## Evidence

### Runtime proof (direct Node.js, no vitest)

`src/agents/tool-loop-detection-runtime-proof.mts` exercises the fix through the
**production `detectToolCallLoop` code path** using `tsx` — no vitest, no mocks.
The test bypasses vitest's module isolation and verifies the real subsystem
logger output.

**Real environment:**
- OS: Linux x86_64, kernel 4.19.112
- Runtime: Node.js v24.13.1, tsx v4.22.3
- Commit: `204c3bd9`

**Exact command:**

```
$ /path/to/tsx src/agents/tool-loop-detection-runtime-proof.mts
```

**Full terminal output:**

```
=== Real runtime proof: failed-exec loop detection (#93917) ===

Prove 1: Repeated docker-ps failures (same failureKind, varying error text)
[agents/loop-detection] Global circuit breaker triggered: exec repeated 30 times with no progress
  stuck=true  level=critical  detector=global_circuit_breaker
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ — circuit breaker triggered

Prove 2: Repeated date calls (completed, varying output)
[agents/loop-detection] Loop warning: exec called 30 times with identical arguments
  stuck=true  level=warning  detector=generic_repeat
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ — varying completed output stays at warning

Prove 3: Mixed failure kinds (timeout/terminated/connection_refused)
[agents/loop-detection] Loop warning: exec called 30 times with identical arguments
  stuck=true  level=warning  detector=generic_repeat
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ — distinct failure modes not merged

Prove 4: Numeric exitSignal (9=SIGKILL) vs string (SIGTERM)
[agents/loop-detection] Loop warning: exec called 30 times with identical arguments
  stuck=true  level=warning  detector=generic_repeat
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ — numeric and string exitSignals kept distinct

=== All runtime proofs complete ===
```

**Observed result after fix:**
- Failed exec with same failureKind + exitCode now fires `global_circuit_breaker` ✅
- Completed exec with varying output stays at `warning` (output IS progress) ✅
- Different failureKind values produce distinct hashes — no false merge ✅
- Numeric exitSignal (9) and string exitSignal ("SIGTERM") produce distinct hashes ✅
- The `[agents/loop-detection]` subsystem logger confirms real production code path

### Unit tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  61 passed (61)
```

**What was not tested:** Live gateway integration with real `exec` tool calls — the
change is pure hash computation; coverage for non-exec tool types is unchanged.
target.author: sunlit-deng
